disputed_language: For a centrally managed Security Hub CSPM target, only the delegated administrator can change the target's Security Hub CSPM configuration across the home and linked Regions.
explanation: Central management makes the delegated administrator authoritative for the settings and operations covered by Security Hub CSPM configuration policies. The artifact says all Security Hub CSPM configuration, although AWS separately limits configuration policies to enablement, standards, controls, and selected control parameters and excludes other configuration domains such as multicloud standards.
required_action: Narrow the statement to settings controlled by central configuration policies and retain the centrally managed/home-and-linked-Region conditions.
suggested_revision: For a centrally managed target, only the delegated administrator can change the Security Hub CSPM enablement, standards, controls, and supported control parameters governed by configuration policies in the home and linked Regions.

supporting_source.locator: Security Hub CSPM configuration policy; Centrally managed; account-specific operations blocked for centrally managed accounts
supporting_source.title: Understanding central configuration in Security Hub CSPM
